definition: Whether covenant-lite structures produce cliff-edge defaults (bimodal distribution, no maintenance covenant early warnings) or smoother restructuring outcomes is a central unresolved question. COVID tested covenant-lite only under unprecedented fiscal/monetary intervention, not organic credit deterioration.
evidence: Maintenance covenants eliminated in covenant-lite structures
COVID natural experiment: covenant-lite recovery rates (65-70 cents) comparable to covenanted loans
COVID confounded by PPP $800B+, Fed corporate bond purchases, and ZIRP
Bimodal thesis specifically requires economic weakness without sufficient policy support — untested
open_questions: Will covenant-lite produce cliff-edge or smoother defaults in organic downturn?
Is the COVID experiment informative given massive policy intervention?
What does the default distribution look like without a policy backstop?
